slides.title: What You Need
slides.body:
To enter RCM you need:

  1. An RCM jig - a small plastic clip that bridges pins 1 and 10 on the right Joy-Con rail. Available cheaply online.

  2. A USB-C cable to connect your Switch to a PC.

  3. A payload injector tool on your PC - TegraRcmGUI (Windows) is the easiest option.

slides.title: Entering RCM
slides.body:
Follow these steps carefully:

  1. Power off your Switch completely.
     (Hold Power -> Power Options -> Power Off)

  2. Remove the right Joy-Con.

  3. Insert the RCM jig into the right Joy-Con rail.

  4. Hold Vol+ and press Power.

The screen will stay black - this is normal. Your Switch is now in RCM and waiting for a payload.
slides.note: If the Nintendo logo appears, you missed RCM. Power off completely and retry.

slides.title: Injecting a Payload
slides.body:
With your Switch in RCM and connected via USB-C:

  1. Open TegraRcmGUI on your PC.
  2. It should detect your Switch automatically (green indicator).
  3. Click the folder icon and select hekate_ctcaer_X.X.X.bin from the Hekate release.
  4. Click Inject Payload.

Hekate will boot and show its menu. From there you can launch Atmosphere, manage partitions, and more.

For future boots, you can use a dedicated RCM dongle instead of a PC.
slides.note: If hekate doesn't launch and the injected size is 0x0000 bytes instead of 0x7000 bytes, your console is patched.
